highlights.title: SWE-Pruner Pro: The Coder LLM Already Knows What to Prune
highlights.summary: SWE-Pruner Pro prunes tool outputs inside the agent, saving up to 39% of prompt and completion tokens.
highlights.detail: This approach preserves task quality and has bounded inference overhead, making it efficient for context management.

highlights.title: FlashRT: Agent Harness for Guiding Agents to Deploy Real-Time Multimodal Applications
highlights.summary: FlashRT guides coding agents to deploy real-time multimodal applications efficiently.
highlights.detail: It uses a chain-of-program paradigm to transform reference implementations into optimized multi-GPU deployments.

highlights.title: LLM-as-a-Coach: Experiential Learning for Non-Verifiable Tasks
highlights.summary: Experiential Learning (EL) provides dense supervision for open-ended tasks using LLM-as-a-Coach.
highlights.detail: EL outperforms rubric-based RL and generalizes better to unseen tasks, preserving fine-grained preferences.

highlights.title: Distributive Laws for Parallel Composition in Rely-Guarantee Concurrency
highlights.summary: Distributive laws for rely-guarantee concurrency developed
highlights.detail: The distributive laws are essential for algebraic manipulation of concurrent programs. The approach restricts the form of the command being distributed to devise stronger equality laws.

highlights.title: Extending Elle for Transaction Workloads with Duplicate Values
highlights.detail: The extension of Elle enables it to handle transaction workloads with duplicate values. This is crucial for real database systems where isolation bugs often manifest.

items.detail: This approach preserves task quality and has bounded inference overhead, making it efficient for context management.

items.detail: It uses a chain-of-program paradigm to transform reference implementations into optimized multi-GPU deployments.

items.title: Jul 20, 2026 Announcements Apply for Anthropic’s AI for Science rare disease research grants
items.summary: Anthropic offers AI for Science rare disease research grants.
items.detail: The grants aim to support research in rare diseases using AI technologies.

items.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
items.summary: OpenAI shares lessons on safety and alignment in long-horizon models.
items.detail: The company highlights new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

items.title: Introducing Cosmos 3 Edge
items.detail: No additional information is available.

items.title: Model: DavidAU/Qwen3.6-27B-Fable-Fusion-711-Uncensored-Heretic-NM-DAU-NEO-MAX-MTP-GGUF
items.detail: The model is an image-text-to-text model with 167 likes.

items.title: Model: empero-ai/Qwythos-9B-Claude-Mythos-5-1M-GGUF
items.detail: The model is an image-text-to-text model with 2373 likes.

items.title: Model: prism-ml/Bonsai-27B-gguf
items.detail: The model is a text-generation model with 546 likes.

items.title: Security updates for Monday
items.summary: Security updates have been issued by Debian, Fedora, and Mageia.
items.detail: The updates address various security vulnerabilities in different packages.

items.title: [$] Merging famfs?
items.summary: Famfs filesystem may be merged into the Linux kernel.
items.detail: The filesystem provides shared access to huge memory-resident files on CXL devices.

items.title: Catanzaro: Some changes to GNOME security tracking
items.summary: GNOME security tracking changes due to AI-generated reports.
items.detail: Michael Catanzaro will step away from managing security issue tracking by December 1, 2026.

items.title: [$] Fedora grapples with change
items.detail: The project is reviewing its well-defined change process for technical changes and major distribution changes. This review aims to determine if the current processes are still effective.

items.title: A Feature-Rich Drum Machine
items.detail: A project featuring a drum machine with various features has been built. The project's details are not fully provided in the given text.

items.title: Detection of a Four-Carbon Sugar in Interstellar Space
items.detail: The detection of a four-carbon sugar in interstellar space may have implications for the formation of life on other planets. The discovery could provide insights into the origins of life.

items.title: Distributive Laws for Parallel Composition in Rely-Guarantee Concurrency
items.summary: Distributive laws for rely-guarantee concurrency developed
items.detail: The distributive laws are essential for algebraic manipulation of concurrent programs. The approach restricts the form of the command being distributed to devise stronger equality laws.

items.title: Extending Elle for Transaction Workloads with Duplicate Values
items.detail: The extension of Elle enables it to handle transaction workloads with duplicate values. This is crucial for real database systems where isolation bugs often manifest.

items.title: Isolation Failure From Shared Storage: Characterizing and Exploiting Page-Cache SCA Leakage Across Containers and VMs
items.summary: Isolation failure from shared storage characterized
items.detail: The shared page cache can form a microarchitectural timing side channel. This channel can be exploited to reveal page-cache residency across isolation boundaries.

items.title: The Duality of Information Flow: Reconciling Robust Downgrading with Non-Interference
items.detail: The reconciliation of robust downgrading with non-interference is achieved through parametric information flow. This approach uses recent insights from modal logic.

items.title: ETAS: An Effect-Typed Language for Agent Systems
items.detail: ETAS treats model-backed agents and other program elements as semantic components. It separates deterministic computation from agentic nondeterminism and externally visible actions.

items.title: Natural Language Access to Domain-Specific Metadata: A Reusable Framework for LLM Query Generation
items.summary: Natural language access to metadata framework developed
items.detail: The Natural Language Knowledge Graph Query system enables natural language access to domain-specific archives. It uses Large Language Models to generate accurate structured queries.

items.title: TabbyML/tabby
items.detail: TabbyML is a self-hosted AI coding assistant. It is built using Rust and provides a self-hosted solution for coding assistance.

items.title: oblien/openship
items.detail: OpenShip is a self-hosted deployment platform. It provides a solution for deploying applications in a self-hosted environment.

items.title: DioxusLabs/dioxus
items.detail: Dioxus is a full-stack app framework for web, desktop, and mobile applications. It is built using Rust and provides a solution for building cross-platform applications.

items.title: biomejs/biome
items.summary: Biome is a toolchain for web projects
items.detail: It provides functionalities to maintain web projects, including a formatter and linter. This can be useful for developers looking to streamline their workflow.

items.title: dmlc/xgboost
items.summary: XGBoost is a gradient boosting library
items.detail: It supports various platforms, including single machines, Hadoop, and Spark, making it a versatile tool for machine learning tasks.

items.title: ggml-org/ggml
items.detail: It is designed for machine learning applications, providing a foundation for building and training models.
